headlines.text: U.S. sends Iran 15-point ceasefire plan via Pakistan
Pentagon deploying up to 3,000 troops to Middle East
Ukraine launches record 389-drone overnight attack on Russia
Philippines declares national energy emergency over Iran war
DHS shutdown tops 1 month; Senate blocks bill again 47–37
Meta ordered to pay $375M in New Mexico child safety verdict
Shaq's alleged Sabrina Carpenter DMs go viral; Shaq denies
Democrat flips Florida state house seat in Mar-a-Lago district

breaking.text: Vance and Rubio leading Iran ceasefire negotiations; Hegseth reportedly 'disappointed' a deal is even being discussed.

stories.hed: U.S. Sends Peace Plan, Hegseth Unhappy About It, Iran Keeps Firing
stories.dek: Vance and Rubio are now leading ceasefire talks; Trump says Iran is willing to listen to 'sustainable' proposals. Hegseth and the Joint Chiefs were reportedly 'quite disappointed' a deal is even on the table. Israel is mobilizing 400,000 reserves anyway. Totally fine.

stories.hed: Ukraine Launches Largest-Ever Drone Attack on Russia — 389 Overnight
stories.dek: The biggest reported drone assault on Russian territory since the war began — hitting 13 regions plus Crimea. Meanwhile Russia is sharing intelligence with Iran, filling its war chest with oil windfalls, and prepping a spring offensive. Everything is fine.

stories.hed: DHS Shutdown Hits One Month; TSA Lines Growing Into Spring Break
stories.dek: Senate blocked the funding bill again, 47–37. Thune threatened to cancel Easter recess. TSA wait times are ballooning right as spring break travel peaks. A new DHS chief was just sworn in, which is fun, because the department still has no money.

stories.hed: Meta Hit with $375M Verdict; Democrat Flips Mar-a-Lago District
stories.dek: A New Mexico jury found Meta negligent in protecting minors from predators — $375M. Separately, a Democrat flipped a Florida state house seat that literally includes Trump's Mar-a-Lago home. Two pieces of news that will live rent-free in Zuckerberg's and Trump's heads all week.

stories.hed: Disney Killed Its $1B OpenAI Deal and Shaq's DMs Are Everyone's Problem Now
stories.dek: Disney quietly pulled out of its $1B Sora/OpenAI deal — the one Bob Iger personally brokered. Meanwhile Shaq went on his own podcast to read aloud alleged DMs to Sabrina Carpenter and deny them simultaneously. One of these stories will matter more to your group chat.

markets.context: Markets rallied on Iran peace plan optimism — oil fell hard, stocks and gold surged.

gas.note: Up $1.02 in March alone — 10th straight weekly increase — and economists say it could wipe out most people's tax refunds. Treat yourself to something nice with the remaining $4.

popCulture.hed: Shaq Read His Alleged Sabrina Carpenter DMs Aloud on His Own Podcast
popCulture.dek: Screenshots of alleged messages from Shaq to Sabrina Carpenter went viral. His response: go on The Big Podcast with Shaq and read them aloud while denying they're real. Bold strategy. Separately, Mormon Wives cast reportedly told ABC execs on a recorded call that they had serious concerns about Taylor Frankie Paul — and ABC exec said he 'didn't want to know too much.'

genZ.definition: Short for delusional — used affectionately to describe someone (often yourself) who has wildly unrealistic hopes or fantasies, usually about a crush or celebrity.
genZ.usage: Me thinking Shaq's DMs were somehow going to be smooth: fully delulu behavior.

tldr: Iran peace talks are happening but nobody — including Hegseth — seems thrilled about it, and both sides kept shooting anyway. Domestically: DHS still broke, Meta owes $375M, and a Democrat just flipped Mar-a-Lago's backyard.
